self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can treat real Discovery monitor artifacts as first-class canonical current heads instead of leaving them behind the routing record.
self_improvement.mechanism: The adopted slice adds a minimal monitor-record reader and links routed monitor state to the live monitor artifact.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP slice, discovery/monitor paths were unsupported and the completed monitor case still pointed at the routing record as the live head.
self_improvement.expected_effect: Current-era Discovery monitor cases will resolve more truthfully without requiring queue precedence redesign.

decision.rationale: The slice is narrow, shared, and closes the remaining current-era monitor truth gap without broadening into queue policy.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
